Class ImageUtil

java.lang.Object
com.aptcoursework.utils.ImageUtil
public class ImageUtil extends Object
Utility class for handling image-related file operations.

This class provides helper methods for uploading, saving, and deleting image files within the application. It is primarily used to manage file storage operations on the server-side.

Supported operations include:

  • Uploading images to a specified directory
  • Validating image file types
  • Deleting images from the file system

This class is designed as a utility class and should not be instantiated.

Author:
Heaven Gurung, Sugam Rana Magar

  • Constructor Details

    • ImageUtil

      public ImageUtil()

  • Method Details

    • userProfilePictureUploader

      public static String userProfilePictureUploader(jakarta.servlet.http.Part partUser, int userID, String uploadPath)
      Uploads and stores a user's profile picture on the server.

      This method creates the upload directory if it does not already exist, generates a new filename based on the user's ID, and saves the uploaded image file to the specified location. Existing files with the same name are replaced.

      If the uploaded file does not contain a valid file extension, a default profile image path is returned instead.

      Parameters:
      partUser - the uploaded file part containing the user's profile image
      userID - the unique identifier of the user
      uploadPath - the absolute path where the image should be stored
      Returns:
      the relative path of the uploaded profile image, or the default profile image path if the upload fails or no valid extension exists
      Throws:
      Exception - no exception is thrown directly, but any file handling exceptions encountered during upload are caught and logged

    • imageUploader

      public static void imageUploader(jakarta.servlet.http.Part part, String filename, String uploadPath)
      Uploads an image file to the specified server directory.

      This method validates the file type, creates the upload directory if it does not exist, and saves the uploaded file to the given path.

      Only files with .jpg or .jpeg extensions are allowed. If the file is invalid, empty, or an error occurs during upload, the method returns null.

      If the upload is successful, the absolute file path of the saved image is returned.

      Parameters:
      part - the uploaded file part from the HTTP request
      filename - the original name of the uploaded file
      uploadPath - the directory path where the file should be saved

    • imageDeleter

      public static void imageDeleter(String filePath)
      Deletes an image file from the file system.

      This method checks whether the provided file path is valid and whether the file exists. If the file exists, it attempts to delete it from the system and logs the result.

      If the file path is null, empty, or the file does not exist, the method simply returns without performing any operation.

      Parameters:
      filePath - the absolute path of the image file to be deleted
